title: Assessment of UV radiation emitted by linear fluorescent lamps in Iran
author: Dehghani, Bazargani, et al.

steps_raw.title: Description of protocol
steps_raw.instruction: Measurements are significant at 2, 5, 20, 50, 150ŌĆēcm (P 0.05). Table 3, Table 4, respectively, show the results of univariate ANOVA of UVC measurement based on distance and time. There was no significant relationship for UV measurements at 5, 50, 150ŌĆēcm (PŌĆē>ŌĆē0.05). Table 4, the pairwise comparison of UVC at different times indicates that there is no significant relationship between the mean of measured values at different times with the mean of the measurements in other times. The maximum and the minimum UVA are 0.321 and 0.006ŌĆēW/m2, respectively, which are below the predefined limits. The highest and the lowest values of UVC radiation emitted from linear fluorescent lamps were 0.049 and 0.002 at 150ŌĆēcm (Table 5, Table 6).The high UVC value is attributed to the defect in bulbsŌĆÖ inner phosphor coating. To compare the UVA radiation intensity, the ANOVA was used. steps_raw.title: Materials and methods
steps_raw.instruction: The study was performed on 40 linear fluorescent lamps in Iran. First, the light intensity of lamps was measured by the Luxmeter (Hagner, Model: EC1). If the difference of light intensity was more than 5% of other lamps, it would be excluded from the study. The watt range of selected lamps was between 11 and 40ŌĆēW [[1], [2], [3], [4], [5], [6], [7]].

materials: The study was performed on 40 linear fluorescent lamps in Iran. First, the light intensity of lamps was measured by the Luxmeter (Hagner, Model: EC1). If the difference of light intensity was more than 5% of other lamps, it would be excluded from the study. The watt range of selected lamps was between 11 and 40ŌĆēW [[1], [2], [3], [4], [5], [6], [7]].
